stories.title: Phillies 14-4 Under Mattingly — Sanchez Shutout, Harper 457-Foot HR Power Philly to .500
stories.angle: NEW STORY. Cristopher Sánchez struck out a career-high 13 batters while throwing the second complete-game shutout of his career as the Phillies beat Pittsburgh 6-0 on Saturday. Bryce Harper crushed a 457-foot three-run HR in the first inning off Bubba Chandler — his longest since 2022. Philadelphia improved to 14-4 under interim manager Don Mattingly and reached .500 for the first time since mid-April. Sánchez has now thrown 29⅔ consecutive scoreless innings. The Phillies are a completely different team under Mattingly.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Cristopher Sánchez: career-high 13 Ks. Second career shutout. 29⅔ scoreless innings.

Bryce Harper: 457-foot 3-run bomb. First inning.

Phillies 6-0 Pirates. 14-4 under Mattingly. Back to .500.

This team is different now. 🔥

#Phillies #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Don Mattingly replaced Rob Thomson as Phillies manager.

Since then: 14-4.

That's a .778 winning percentage. With the same roster.

Saturday was the showcase: Sanchez fans 13, Harper goes 457 feet.

What a turnaround. 📈

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Sanchez Fans 13, Harper Bombs 457 Feet: Phillies 6-0 Pirates and 14-4 Under Mattingly

stories.title: Schwarber's 7-Game HR Streak Ends — But 20 HRs on Earliest Date in MLB History
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Schwarber 7-game streak covered May 16). Kyle Schwarber's consecutive-games HR streak ended at 7 Saturday without tying Don Mattingly's MLB record of 8. But the milestone remains extraordinary: Schwarber's 20th homer (hit May 15) set the MLB record for earliest date to reach 20 HRs in a season, breaking Luis Gonzalez's mark from May 17, 2001. He still leads all of baseball with 20 HRs.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber's 7-game HR streak ended Saturday.

He never tied Mattingly's 8-game MLB record — which his own manager holds. 😅

But the history was already made:

20 HRs by May 15 = earliest date in MLB history. Ever.

Still leads baseball with 20. 💣

#Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber's 7-Game HR Streak Ends — But He Already Made the Most Historic Record of All

stories.title: Juan Soto's 250th HR Enters MLB History — But Mets Worried About His Wrist
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Soto 250th HR covered May 16). More detail on Soto's historic milestone: at 27 years old, he's the youngest player to reach 250 career HRs since Albert Pujols, and joins Alex Rodriguez, Ken Griffey Jr., Mickey Mantle and Mike Trout as the only players in history with 250+ HRs and 95+ SBs before turning 28. But he was grimacing with wrist discomfort in the same game, raising health concerns for the Mets' franchise player. Mets lost 5-2 to the Yankees in Game 1 of the Subway Series.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Juan Soto hit his 250th career HR Friday.

393 feet. 102.6 mph exit velocity. Off Schlittler in the 7th.

Youngest since Pujols to reach 250 HRs.

He was also grimacing with wrist discomfort three innings earlier.

Two stories in one. 😬

#Mets #MLB

article.headline: Soto's 250th HR Joins Baseball's Most Elite History — But Mets Are Watching His Wrist

stories.title: Cam Schlittler's Walter Johnson Start — The Most Historic Pitching Season in Over 100 Years
stories.angle: FOLLOW UP (Schlittler AL ERA leader covered May 16). Through his first 9 starts, Cam Schlittler is posting numbers not seen since Walter Johnson in 1913: 1.35 ERA, 5-1 record, 59 strikeouts, just 9 walks, and only 1 HR allowed. WHIP of 0.81. He is the first pitcher in over 110 years to combine those metrics through the first 9 starts of a season. The 25-year-old Yankees starter is in the middle of one of the most historically dominant pitching seasons in modern baseball.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Cam Schlittler through 9 starts:

1.35 ERA. 5-1. 59 Ks. 9 BB. 1 HR allowed. 0.81 WHIP.

The last pitcher to match those numbers through his first 9 starts?

Walter Johnson. 1913.

That was 113 years ago. 😳

#Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Cam Schlittler's Numbers Haven't Been Seen Since Walter Johnson in 1913

stories.title: Braves (30-14) Own MLB's Best Record — Weekend Standings Check
stories.angle: NEW STORY. The Atlanta Braves (30-14) have the best record in baseball as the weekend wraps up, leading the NL East by a wide margin. The Chicago Cubs (28-16) lead the NL Central. In the AL, the Tampa Bay Rays (29-14) lead the AL East. The Phillies' surge under Mattingly (14-4) has brought them to .500 — the biggest managerial impact story of the 2026 season. A look at the league landscape as teams head into the final weeks of May.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Weekend MLB standings check:

🔴 Braves: 30-14 (best in baseball)
🔵 Cubs: 28-16 (NL Central)
🍋 Rays: 29-14 (AL East)
🔴 Phillies: at .500 (14-4 under Mattingly)

The races are forming. Who do you have winning their division? 👇

#MLB #Baseball

stories.title: MLB Sunday Round-Up — Subway Series Finale and a Packed Weekend in Review
stories.angle: NEW STORY. Sunday's MLB slate features the Subway Series finale (Yankees vs. Mets at Citi Field, 1:40 PM ET), plus action across all divisions. Weekend highlights: Phillies 6-0 Pirates (Sanchez/Harper); Cubs won Crosstown Classic over White Sox; Braves strong in NL East. A recap of what the weekend brought and what to watch Sunday.

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
MLB weekend wrap:

🗽 Subway Series finale at Citi Field today
💣 Phillies 6-0 Pirates (Sanchez 13 Ks / Harper 457 ft)
💍 Schwarber's 7-game HR streak ended — but 20 HRs fastest ever
🏟️ Soto 250th HR — with wrist concerns

Sunday baseball waits for no one. ⚾

#MLB #Baseball
